death.cause: Heart attack while walking on the Champs-Élysées

narrative_blocks.header: Heart attack on Champs-Élysées morning walk, 1909
narrative_blocks.body: He died on 4 July 1909 while taking a morning walk on the Champs-Élysées in Paris at age sixty-five. The Stuyvesant and Rutherfurd family holdings in New York he had spent a lifetime managing passed to his two Dutch-born sons by his second wife.

narrative_blocks.header: Renamed at six to inherit Stuyvesant fortune
narrative_blocks.body: Born John Stuyvesant Rutherfurd in Manhattan in 1843, he was renamed 'Rutherfurd Stuyvesant' at age six per the will of his grand-uncle Peter Gerard Stuyvesant. The renaming preserved the Stuyvesant surname across a female-line succession into the next generation.

narrative_blocks.header: Built first true NYC apartment building, 1869
narrative_blocks.body: In 1869 he hired architect Richard Morris Hunt to build what the New York Tribune called 'the first true apartment building in New York' at 142 East 18th Street near Gramercy Park. The five-story walkup for middle-class tenants was modeled on Parisian apartments and described as 'wholly different from the plan of the tenement house.'

narrative_blocks.header: Built memorial chapel after wife died in childbirth
narrative_blocks.body: His first wife Mary Rutherfurd Pierrepont died in childbirth on 31 December 1879; the child did not survive. In her memory he built the Memorial Chapel of St Mark's Parish at 288 East 10th Street Manhattan in 1882-83.
